title: veto-ladder energy acceptance calibration
split: grouped 5-fold complete-run held-out over analysis runs
finding: The support selector winner is new_residual_gated_ensemble with nominal energy-proxy res68 0.00675 [0.00610, 0.00727] at acceptance 0.447.  The transparent ladder gives res68 0.00689 at acceptance 0.444, while the shuffled-target HGB control gives 0.01089.  The result supports treating the veto ladder as a support-acceptance calibration: it changes charge composition and protects low-support regions, but it should not be promoted to an absolute energy or PID improvement without external truth.
